translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Book of the generation of Jesus Christ, Son of David, Son of Abraham.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Abraham begat Isaac; and Isaac begat Jacob, and Jacob begat Juda and his brethren;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Juda begat Phares and Zara of Thamar; and Phares begat Esrom, and Esrom begat Aram,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Aram begat Aminadab, and Aminadab begat Naasson, and Naasson begat Salmon,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Salmon begat Booz of Rachab; and Booz begat Obed of Ruth; and Obed begat Jesse,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Jesse begat David the king. And David begat Solomon, of her [that had been the wife] of Urias;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Solomon begat Roboam, and Roboam begat Abia, and Abia begat Asa,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Asa begat Josaphat, and Josaphat begat Joram, and Joram begat Ozias,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Ozias begat Joatham, and Joatham begat Achaz, and Achaz begat Ezekias,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Ezekias begat Manasses, and Manasses begat Amon, and Amon begat Josias,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Josias begat Jechonias and his brethren, at the time of the carrying away of Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after the carrying away of Babylon, Jechonias begat Salathiel, and Salathiel begat Zorobabel,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Zorobabel begat Abiud, and Abiud begat Eliakim, and Eliakim begat Azor,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Azor begat Sadoc, and Sadoc begat Achim, and Achim begat Eliud,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Eliud begat Eliazar, and Eliazar begat Matthan, and Matthan begat Jacob,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Jacob begat Joseph, the husband of Mary, of whom was born Jesus, who is called Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the generations, therefore, from Abraham to David [were] fourteen generations; and from David until the carrying away of Babylon, fourteen generations; and from the carrying away of Babylon unto the Christ, fourteen generations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the birth of Jesus Christ was thus: His mother, Mary, that is, having been betrothed to Joseph, before they came together, she was found to be with child of [the] Holy Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Joseph, her husband, being [a] righteous [man], and unwilling to expose her publicly, purposed to have put her away secretly;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but while he pondered on these things, behold, an angel of [the] Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, Joseph, son of David, fear not to take to [thee] Mary, thy wife, for that which is begotten in her is of [the] Holy Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she shall bring forth a son, and thou shalt call his name Jesus, for he shall save his people from their sins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now all this came to pass that that might be fulfilled which was spoken by [the] Lord, through the prophet, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, the virgin shall be with child, and shall bring forth a son, and they shall call his name Emmanuel, which is, being interpreted, 'God with us.'

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Joseph, having awoke up from his sleep, did as the angel of [the] Lord had enjoined him, and took to [him] his wife,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and knew her not until she had brought forth her firstborn son: and he called his name Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now Jesus having been born in Bethlehem of Judaea, in the days of Herod the king, behold magi from the east arrived at Jerusalem, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Where is the king of the Jews that has been born? for we have seen his star in the east, and have come to do him homage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Herod the king having heard [of it], was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and, assembling all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he inquired of them where the Christ should be born.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to him, In Bethlehem of Judaea; for thus it is written through the prophet:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou Bethlehem, land of Juda, art in no wise the least among the governors of Juda; for out of thee shall go forth a leader who shall shepherd my people Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Herod, having secretly called the magi, inquired of them accurately the time of the star that was appearing;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having sent them to Bethlehem, said, Go, search out accurately concerning the child, and when ye shall have found [him] bring me back word, so that I also may come and do him homage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they having heard the king went their way; and lo, the star, which they had seen in the east, went before them until it came and stood over the place where the little child was.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they saw the star they rejoiced with exceeding great joy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having come into the house they saw the little child with Mary his mother, and falling down did him homage. And having opened their treasures, they offered to him gifts, gold, and frankincense, and myrrh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And being divinely instructed in a dream not to return to Herod, they departed into their own country another way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now, they having departed, behold, an angel of [the] Lord appears in a dream to Joseph, saying, Arise, take to [thee] the little child and his mother, and flee into Egypt, and be there until I shall tell thee; for Herod will seek the little child to destroy it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, having arisen, he took to [him] the little child and his mother by night, and departed into Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he was there until the death of Herod, that that might be fulfilled which was spoken by [the] Lord through the prophet, saying, Out of Egypt have I called my son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Herod, seeing that he had been mocked by the magi, was greatly enraged; and sent and slew all the boys which [were] in Bethlehem, and in all its borders, from two years and under, according to the time which he had accurately inquired from the magi.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then was fulfilled that which was spoken through Jeremias the prophet, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A voice has been heard in Rama, weeping, and great lamentation: Rachel weeping [for] her children, and would not be comforted, because they are not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Herod having died, behold, an angel of [the] Lord appears in a dream to Joseph in Egypt, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise, take to [thee] the little child and its mother, and go into the land of Israel: for they who sought the life of the little child are dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he arose and took to [him] the little child and its mother, and came into the land of Israel;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but having heard that 'Archelaus reigns over Judaea, instead of Herod his father,' he was afraid to go there; and having been divinely instructed in a dream, he went away into the parts of Galilee,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and came and dwelt in a town called Nazareth; so that that should be fulfilled which was spoken through the prophets, He shall be called a Nazaraene.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now in those days comes John the baptist, preaching in the wilderness of Judaea,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and saying, Repent, for the kingdom of the heavens has drawn nigh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this is he who has been spoken of through Esaias the prophet, saying, Voice of him that crieth in the wilderness: prepare ye the way of [the] Lord, make straight his paths.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And John himself had his garment of camel's hair, and a leathern girdle about his loins, and his nourishment was locusts and wild honey.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then went out to him Jerusalem, and all Judaea, and all the country round the Jordan,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and were baptised by him in the Jordan, confessing their sins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But seeing many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to his baptism, he said to them, Offspring of vipers, who has forewarned you to flee from the coming wrath?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Produce therefore fruit worthy of repentance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And do not think to say within yourselves, We have Abraham for [our] father; for I say unto you, thatGod is able of these stones to raise up children to Abraham.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And already the axe is applied to the root of the trees; every tree therefore not producing good fruit is cut down and cast into the fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I indeed baptise you with water to repentance, but he that comes after me is mightier than I, whose sandals I am not fit to bear; he shall baptise you with [the] Holy Spirit and fire;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: whose winnowing fan [is] in his hand, and he shall thoroughly purge his threshing-floor, and shall gather his wheat into the garner, but the chaff he will burn with fire unquenchable.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then comes Jesus from Galilee to the Jordan to John, to be baptised of him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but John urgently forbad him, saying, I have need to be baptised of thee; and comest thou to me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus answering said to him, Suffer [it] now; for thus it becometh us to fulfil all righteousness. Then he suffers him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, having been baptised, went up straightway from the water, and lo, the heavens were opened to him, and he saw the Spirit ofGod descending as a dove, and coming upon him:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and behold, a voice out of the heavens saying, This is my beloved Son, in whom I have found my delight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Jesus was carried up into the wilderness by the Spirit to be tempted of the devil:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having fasted forty days and forty nights, afterwards he hungered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the tempter coming up to him said, If thou be Son ofGod, speak, that these stones may become loaves of bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering said, It is written, Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word which goes out throughGod's mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the devil takes him to the holy city, and sets him upon the edge of the temple,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and says to him, If thou be Son ofGod cast thyself down; for it is written, He shall give charge to his angels concerning thee, and on [their] hands shall they bear thee, lest in anywise thou strike thy foot against a stone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to him, It is again written, Thou shalt not tempt [the] Lord thyGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Again the devil takes him to a very high mountain, and shews him all the kingdoms of the world, and their glory,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and says to him, All these things will I give thee if, falling down, thou wilt do me homage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then says Jesus to him, Get thee away, Satan, for it is written, Thou shalt do homage to [the] Lord thyGod, and him alone shalt thou serve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the devil leaves him, and behold, angels came and ministered to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But having heard that John was delivered up, he departed into Galilee:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having left Nazareth, he went and dwelt at Capernaum, which is on the sea-side in the borders of Zabulon and Nepthalim,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that that might be fulfilled which was spoken through Esaias the prophet, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Land of Zabulon and land of Nepthalim, way of [the] sea beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the nations:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — the people sitting in darkness has seen a great light, and to those sitting in [the] country and shadow of death, to them has light sprung up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From that time began Jesus to preach and to say, Repent, for the kingdom of the heavens has drawn nigh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And walking by the sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers, Simon called Peter, and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea, for they were fishers;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he says to them, Come after me, and I will make you fishers of men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they, having left their trawl-nets, immediately followed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And going on thence he saw other two brothers, James the [son] of Zebedee and John his brother, in the ship with Zebedee their father, mending their trawl-nets, and he called them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they, having left the ship and their father, immediately followed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [Jesus] went round the whole [of] Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the glad tidings of the kingdom, and healing every disease and every bodily weakness among the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his fame went out into the whole [of] Syria, and they brought to him all that were ill, suffering under various diseases and pains, and those possessed by demons, and lunatics, and paralytics; and he healed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And great crowds followed him from Galilee, and Decapolis, and Jerusalem, and Judaea, and beyond the Jordan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But seeing the crowds, he went up into the mountain, and having sat down, his disciples came to him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and, having opened his mouth, he taught them, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed [are] the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed they that mourn, for they shall be comforted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed they who hunger and thirst after righteousness, for they shall be filled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed the merciful, for they shall find mercy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed the pure in heart, for they shall seeGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed the peace-makers, for they shall be called sons ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed they who are persecuted on account of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed are ye when they may reproach and persecute you, and say every wicked thing against you, lying, for my sake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Rejoice and exult, for your reward is great in the heavens; for thus have they persecuted the prophets who were before you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye are the salt of the earth; but if the salt have become insipid, wherewith shall it be salted? It is no longer fit for anything but to be cast out and to be trodden under foot by men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye are the light of the world: a city situated on the top of a mountain cannot be hid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Nor do [men] light a lamp and put it under the bushel, but upon the lamp-stand, and it shines for all who are in the house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let your light thus shine before men, so that they may see your upright works, and glorify your Father who is in the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Think not that I am come to make void the law or the prophets; I am not come to make void, but to fulfil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For verily I say unto you, Until the heaven and the earth pass away, one iota or one tittle shall in no wise pass from the law till all come to pass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whosoever then shall do away with one of these least commandments, and shall teach men so, shall be called least in the kingdom of the heavens; but whosoever shall practise and teach [them], he shall be called great in the kingdom of the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I say unto you, that unless your righteousness surpass [that] of the scribes and Pharisees, ye shall in no wise enter into the kingdom of the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye have heard that it was said to the ancients, Thou shalt not kill; but whosoever shall kill shall be subject to the judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say unto you, that every one that is lightly angry with his brother shall be subject to the judgment; but whosoever shall say to his brother, Raca, shall be subject to [be called before] the sanhedrim; but whosoever shall say, Fool, shall be subject to the penalty of the hell of fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If therefore thou shouldest offer thy gift at the altar, and there shouldest remember that thy brother has something against thee,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: leave there thy gift before the altar, and first go, be reconciled to thy brother, and then come and offer thy gift.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Make friends with thine adverse party quickly, whilst thou art in the way with him; lest some time the adverse party deliver thee to the judge, and the judge deliver thee to the officer, and thou be cast into prison.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily I say to thee, Thou shalt in no wise come out thence till thou hast paid the last farthing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye have heard that it has been said, Thou shalt not commit adultery.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say unto you, that every one who looks upon a woman to lust after her has already committed adultery with her in his heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if thy right eye be a snare to thee, pluck it out and cast it from thee: for it is profitable for thee that one of thy members perish, and not thy whole body be cast into hell.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if thy right hand be a snare to thee, cut it off and cast it from thee: for it is profitable for thee that one of thy members perish, and not thy whole body be cast into hell.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It has been said too, Whosoever shall put away his wife, let him give her a letter of divorce.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say unto you, that whosoever shall put away his wife, except for cause of fornication, makes her commit adultery, and whosoever marries one that is put away commits adultery.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Again, ye have heard that it has been said to the ancients, Thou shalt not forswear thyself, but shalt render to the Lord what thou hast sworn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say unto you, Do not swear at all; neither by the heaven, because it is [the] throne ofGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: nor by the earth, because it is [the] footstool of his feet; nor by Jerusalem, because it is [the] city of the great King.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Neither shalt thou swear by thy head, because thou canst not make one hair white or black.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But let your word be Yea, yea; Nay, nay; but what is more than these is from evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye have heard that it has been said, Eye for eye and tooth for tooth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say unto you, not to resist evil; but whoever shall strike thee on thy right cheek, turn to him also the other;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to him that would go to law with thee and take thy body coat, leave him thy cloak also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And whoever will compel thee to go one mile, go with him two.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To him that asks of thee give, and from him that desires to borrow of thee turn not away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye have heard that it has been said, Thou shalt love thy neighbour and hate thine enemy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say unto you, Love your enemies, [bless those who curse you,] do good to those who hate you, and pray for those who [insult you and] persecute you,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that ye may be [the] sons of your Father who is in [the] heavens; for he makes his sun rise on evil and good, and sends rain on just and unjust.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if ye should love those who love you, what reward have ye? Do not also the tax-gatherers the same?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if ye should salute your brethren only, what do ye extraordinary? Do not also the Gentiles the same?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be ye therefore perfect as your heavenly Father is perfect.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take heed not to do your alms before men to be seen of them, otherwise ye have no reward with your Father who is in the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When therefore thou doest alms, sound not a trumpet before thee, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, so that they may have glory from men. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou, when thou doest alms, let not thy left hand know what thy right hand does;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so that thine alms may be in secret, and thy Father who sees in secret will render [it] to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when thou prayest, thou shalt not be as the hypocrites; for they love to pray standing in the synagogues and in the corners of the streets so that they should appear to men. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou, when thou prayest, enter into thy chamber, and having shut thy door, pray to thy Father who is in secret, and thy Father who sees in secret will render [it] to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when ye pray, use not vain repetitions, as those who are of the nations: for they think they shall be heard through their much speaking.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not ye therefore like them, for your Father knows of what things ye have need before ye beg [anything] of him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus therefore pray ye: Our Father who art in the heavens, let thy name be sanctified,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: let thy kingdom come, let thy will be done as in heaven so upon the earth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: give us to-day our needed bread,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and lead us not into temptation, but save us from evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if ye forgive men their offences, your heavenly Father also will forgive you [yours],

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but if ye do not forgive men their offences, neither will your Father forgive your offences.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when ye fast, be not as the hypocrites, downcast in countenance; for they disfigure their faces, so that they may appear fasting to men: verily I say unto you, They have their reward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou, [when] fasting, anoint thy head and wash thy face,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so that thou mayest not appear fasting unto men, but to thy Father who is in secret; and thy Father who sees in secret shall render [it] to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lay not up for yourselves treasures upon the earth, where moth and rust spoils, and where thieves dig through and steal;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust spoils, and where thieves do not dig through nor steal;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for where thy treasure is, there will be also thy heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The lamp of the body is the eye; if therefore thine eye be single, thy whole body will be light:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but if thine eye be wicked, thy whole body will be dark. If therefore the light that is in thee be darkness, how great the darkness!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: No one can serve two masters; for either he will hate the one and will love the other, or he will hold to the one and despise the other. Ye cannot serveGod and mammon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this cause I say unto you, Do not be careful about your life, what ye should eat and what ye should drink; nor for your body what ye should put on. Is not the life more than food, and the body than raiment?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Look at the birds of the heaven, that they sow not, nor reap, nor gather into granaries, and your heavenly Father nourishes them. Are ye not much more excellent than they?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But which of you by carefulness can add to his growth one cubit?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And why are ye careful about clothing? Observe with attention the lilies of the field, how they grow: they toil not, neither do they spin;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but I say unto you, that not even Solomon in all his glory was clothed as one of these.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ifGod so clothe the herbage of the field, which is to-day, and to-morrow is cast into [the] oven, will he not much rather you, O [ye] of little faith?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not therefore careful, saying, What shall we eat? or What shall we drink? or What shall we put on?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for all these things the nations seek after; for your heavenly Father knows that ye have need of all these things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But seek ye first the kingdom ofGod and his righteousness, and all these things shall be added unto you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not careful therefore for the morrow, for the morrow shall be careful about itself. Sufficient to the day [is] its own evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Judge not, that ye may not be judged;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for with what judgment ye judge, ye shall be judged; and with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But why lookest thou on the mote that is in the eye of thy brother, but observest not the beam that is in thine eye?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or how wilt thou say to thy brother, Allow [me], I will cast out the mote from thine eye; and behold, the beam is in thine eye?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hypocrite, cast out first the beam out of thine eye, and then thou wilt see clearly to cast out the mote out of the eye of thy brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give not that which is holy to the dogs, nor cast your pearls before the swine, lest they trample them with their feet, and turning round rend you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ask, and it shall be given to you. Seek, and ye shall find. Knock, and it shall be opened to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For every one that asks receives; and he that seeks finds; and to him that knocks it shall be opened.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or what man is there of you who, if his son shall ask of him a loaf of bread, will give him a stone;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and if he ask a fish, will give him a serpent?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If therefore ye, being wicked, know [how] to give good gifts to your children, how much rather shall your Father who is in the heavens give good things to them that ask of him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore all things whatever ye desire that men should do to you, thus do ye also do to them; for this is the law and the prophets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Enter in through the narrow gate, for wide the gate and broad the way that leads to destruction, and many are they who enter in through it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For narrow the gate and straitened the way that leads to life, and they are few who find it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But beware of false prophets, which come to you in sheep's clothing, but within are ravening wolves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By their fruits ye shall know them. Do [men] gather a bunch of grapes from thorns, or from thistles figs?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So every good tree produces good fruits, but the worthless tree produces bad fruits.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A good tree cannot produce bad fruits, nor a worthless tree produce good fruits.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every tree not producing good fruit is cut down and cast into the fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By their fruits then surely ye shall know them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Not every one who says to me, Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdom of the heavens, but he that does the will of my Father who is in the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Many shall say to me in that day, Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied through thy name, and through thy name cast out demons, and through thy name done many works of power?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and then will I avow unto them, I never knew you. Depart from me, workers of lawlessness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoever therefore hears these my words and does them, I will liken him to a prudent man, who built his house upon the rock;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the rain came down, and the streams came, and the winds blew and fell upon that house, and it did not fall, for it had been founded upon the rock.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And every one who hears these my words and does not do them, he shall be likened to a foolish man, who built his house upon the sand;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the rain came down, and the streams came, and the winds blew and beat upon that house, and it fell, and its fall was great.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Jesus had finished these words, the crowds were astonished at his doctrine,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for he taught them as having authority, and not as their scribes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he had come down from the mountain, great crowds followed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, a leper came up to [him] and did him homage, saying, Lord, if thou wilt, thou art able to cleanse me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he stretched out his hand and touched him, saying, I will; be cleansed. And immediately his leprosy was cleansed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus says to him, See thou tell no man, but go, shew thyself to the priest, and offer the gift which Moses ordained, for a testimony to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he had entered into Capernaum, a centurion came to him, beseeching him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and saying, Lord, my servant lies paralytic in the house, suffering grievously.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus says to him, I will come and heal him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the centurion answered and said, Lord, I am not fit that thou shouldest enter under my roof; but only speak a word, and my servant shall be healed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I also am a man under authority, having under me soldiers, and I say to this [one], Go, and he goes; and to another, Come, and he comes; and to my bondman, Do this, and he does it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Jesus heard it, he wondered, and said to those who followed, Verily I say unto you, Not even in Israel have I found so great faith.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say unto you, that many shall come from [the] rising and setting [sun], and shall lie down at table with Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob in the kingdom of the heavens;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but the sons of the kingdom shall be cast out into the outer darkness: there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to the centurion, Go, and as thou hast believed, be it to thee. And his servant was healed in that hour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Jesus had come to Peter's house, he saw his mother-in-law laid down and in a fever;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he touched her hand, and the fever left her, and she arose and served him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the evening was come, they brought to him many possessed by demons, and he cast out the spirits with a word, and healed all that were ill;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so that that should be fulfilled which was spoken through Esaias the prophet, saying, Himself took our infirmities and bore our diseases.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, seeing great crowds around him, commanded to depart to the other side.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a scribe came up and said to him, Teacher, I will follow thee whithersoever thou mayest go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus says to him, The foxes have holes, and the birds of the heaven roosting-places; but the Son of man has not where he may lay his head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But another of his disciples said to him, Lord, suffer me first to go away and bury my father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said to him, Follow me, and leave the dead to bury their own dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went on board ship and his disciples followed him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and behold, [the water] became very agitated on the sea, so that the ship was covered by the waves; but he slept.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the disciples came and awoke him, saying, Lord save: we perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he says to them, Why are ye fearful, O ye of little faith? Then, having arisen, he rebuked the winds and the sea, and there was a great calm.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the men were astonished, saying, What sort [of man] is this, that even the winds and the sea obey him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there met him, when he came to the other side, to the country of the Gergesenes, two possessed by demons, coming out of the tombs, exceeding dangerous, so that no one was able to pass by that way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, they cried out, saying, What have we to do with thee, Son ofGod? hast thou come here before the time to torment us?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now there was, a great way off from them, a herd of many swine feeding;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the demons besought him, saying, If thou cast us out, send us away into the herd of swine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Go. And they, going out, departed into the herd of swine; and lo, the whole herd [of swine] rushed down the steep slope into the sea, and died in the waters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they that fed them fled, and went away into the city and related everything, and what had happened as to those possessed by demons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, the whole city went out to meet Jesus; and when they saw him, they begged [him] to go away out of their coasts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And going on board the ship, he passed over and came to his own city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, they brought to him a paralytic, laid upon a bed; and Jesus, seeing their faith, said to the paralytic, Be of good courage, child; thy sins are forgiven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, certain of the scribes said to themselves, This [man] blasphemes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, seeing their thoughts, said, Why do ye think evil things in your hearts?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For which is easier: to say, Thy sins are forgiven; or to say, Rise up and walk?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But that ye may know that the Son of man has power on earth to forgive sins, (then he says to the paralytic,) Rise up, take up thy bed and go to thy house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he rose up and went to his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the crowds seeing [it], were in fear, and glorifiedGod who gave such power to men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, passing on thence, saw a man sitting at the tax-office, called Matthew, and says to him, Follow me. And he rose up and followed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, as he lay at table in the house, that behold, many tax-gatherers and sinners came and lay at table with Jesus and his disciples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Pharisees seeing [it], said to his disciples, Why does your teacher eat with tax-gatherers and sinners?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But [Jesus] hearing it, said, They that are strong have not need of a physician, but those that are ill.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But go and learn what [that] is — I will have mercy and not sacrifice; for I have not come to call righteous [men] but sinners.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then come to him the disciples of John, saying, Why do we and the Pharisees often fast, but thy disciples fast not?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to them, Can the sons of the bridechamber mourn so long as the bridegroom is with them? But days will come when the bridegroom will have been taken away from them, and then they will fast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But no one puts a patch of new cloth on an old garment, for its filling up takes from the garment and a worse rent takes place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Nor do men put new wine into old skins, otherwise the skins burst and the wine is poured out, and the skins will be destroyed; but they put new wine into new skins, and both are preserved together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As he spoke these things to them, behold, a ruler coming in did homage to him, saying, My daughter has by this died; but come and lay thy hand upon her and she shall live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus rose up and followed him, and [so did] his disciples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, a woman, who had had a bloody flux [for] twelve years, came behind and touched the hem of his garment;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for she said within herself, If I should only touch his garment I shall be healed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus turning and seeing her, said, Be of good courage, daughter; thy faith has healed thee. And the woman was healed from that hour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when Jesus was come to the house of the ruler, and saw the flute-players and the crowd making a tumult,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he said, Withdraw, for the damsel is not dead, but sleeps. And they derided him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when the crowd had been put out, he went in and took her hand; and the damsel rose up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the fame of it went out into all that land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as Jesus passed on thence, two blind [men] followed him, crying and saying, Have mercy on us, Son of David.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he was come to the house, the blind [men] came to him. And Jesus says to them, Do ye believe that I am able to do this? They say to him, Yea, Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he touched their eyes, saying, According to your faith, be it unto you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their eyes were opened; and Jesus charged them sharply, saying, See, let no man know it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they, when they were gone out, spread his name abroad in all that land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as these were going out, behold, they brought to him a dumb man possessed by a demon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the demon having been cast out, the dumb spake. And the crowds were astonished, saying, It has never been seen thus in Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the Pharisees said, He casts out the demons through the prince of the demons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus went round all the cities and the villages, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the glad tidings of the kingdom, and healing every disease and every bodily weakness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when he saw the crowds he was moved with compassion for them, because they were harassed, and cast away as sheep not having a shepherd.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then saith he to his disciples, The harvest [is] great and the workmen [are] few;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: supplicate therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he send forth workmen unto his harvest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having called to [him] his twelve disciples, he gave them power over unclean spirits, so that they should cast them out, and heal every disease and every bodily weakness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the names of the twelve apostles are these: first, Simon, who was called Peter, and Andrew his brother; James the [son] of Zebedee, and John his brother;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Philip and Bartholomew; Thomas, and Matthew the tax-gatherer; James the [son] of Alphaeus, and Lebbaeus, who was surnamed Thaddaeus;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Simon the Cananaean, and Judas the Iscariote, who also delivered him up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These twelve Jesus sent out when he had charged them, saying, Go not off into [the] way of [the] nations, and into a city of Samaritans enter ye not;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as ye go, preach, saying, The kingdom of the heavens has drawn nigh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Heal [the] infirm, [raise the dead], cleanse lepers, cast out demons: ye have received gratuitously, give gratuitously.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do not provide yourselves with gold, or silver, or brass, for your belts,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: nor scrip for the way, nor two body coats, nor sandals, nor a staff: for the workman is worthy of his nourishment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But into whatsoever city or village ye enter, inquire who in it is worthy, and there remain till ye go forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as ye enter into a house salute it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if the house indeed be worthy, let your peace come upon it; but if it be not worthy, let your peace return to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear your words, as ye go forth out of that house or city, shake off the dust of your feet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrha in judgment-day than for that city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I send you as sheep in the midst of wolves; be therefore prudent as the serpents, and guileless as the doves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But beware of men; for they will deliver you up to sanhedrims, and scourge you in their synagogues;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye shall be brought before rulers and kings for my sake, for a testimony to them and to the nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when they deliver you up, be not careful how or what ye shall speak; for it shall be given to you in that hour what ye shall speak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ye are not the speakers, but the Spirit of your Father which speaks in you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But brother shall deliver up brother to death, and father child; and children shall rise up against parents and shall put them to death;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye shall be hated of all on account of my name. But he that has endured to [the] end, he shall be saved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when they persecute you in this city, flee to the other; for verily I say to you, Ye shall not have completed the cities of Israel until the Son of man be come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The disciple is not above his teacher, nor the bondman above his lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [It is] sufficient for the disciple that he should become as his teacher, and the bondman as his lord. If they have called the master of the house Beelzebub, how much more those of his household?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fear them not therefore; for there is nothing covered which shall not be revealed, and secret which shall not be known.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What I say to you in darkness speak in the light, and what ye hear in the ear preach upon the houses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And be not afraid of those who kill the body, but cannot kill the soul; but fear rather him who is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Are not two sparrows sold for a farthing? and one of them shall not fall to the ground without your Father;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but of you even the hairs of the head are all numbered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fear not therefore; ye are better than many sparrows.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Every one therefore who shall confess me before men, I also will confess him before my Father who is in [the] heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But whosoever shall deny me before men, him will I also deny before my Father who is in [the] heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do not think that I have come to send peace upon the earth: I have not come to send peace, but a sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I have come to set a man at variance with his father, and the daughter with her mother, and the daughter-in-law with her mother-in-law;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they of his household [shall be] a man's enemies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He who loves father or mother above me is not worthy of me; and he who loves son or daughter above me is not worthy of me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he who does not take up his cross and follow after me is not worthy of me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that finds his life shall lose it, and he who has lost his life for my sake shall find it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that receives you receives me, and he that receives me receives him that sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that receives a prophet in the name of a prophet, shall receive a prophet's reward; and he that receives a righteous man in the name of a righteous man, shall receive a righteous man's reward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And whosoever shall give to drink to one of these little ones a cup of cold [water] only, in the name of a disciple, verily I say unto you, he shall in no wise lose his reward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Jesus had finished commanding his twelve disciples, he departed thence to teach and preach in their cities.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But John, having heard in the prison the works of the Christ, sent by his disciples,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said to him, Art thou the coming [one]? or are we to wait for another?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, Go, report to John what ye hear and see.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blind [men] see and lame walk; lepers are cleansed, and deaf hear; and dead are raised, and poor have glad tidings preached to them:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and blessed is whosoever shall not be offended in me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as they went [away], Jesus began to say to the crowds concerning John, What went ye out into the wilderness to see? a reed moved about by the wind?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But what went ye out to see? a man clothed in delicate raiment? behold, those who wear delicate things are in the houses of kings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But what went ye out to see? a prophet? Yea, I say to you, and more than a prophet:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: this is he of whom it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, who shall prepare thy way before thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily I say to you, that there is not arisen among [the] born of women a greater than John the baptist. But he who is a little one in the kingdom of the heavens is greater than he.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But from the days of John the baptist until now, the kingdom of the heavens is taken by violence, and [the] violent seize on it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For all the prophets and the law have prophesied unto John.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if ye will receive it, this is Elias, who is to come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that has ears to hear, let him hear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But to whom shall I liken this generation? It is like children sitting in the markets, which, calling to their companions,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: say, We have piped to you, and ye have not danced: we have mourned to you, and ye have not wailed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For John has come neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He has a demon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Son of man has come eating and drinking, and they say, Behold, a man [that is] eating and wine-drinking, a friend of tax-gatherers, and of sinners: — and wisdom has been justified by her children.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then began he to reproach the cities in which most of his works of power had taken place, because they had not repented.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe to thee, Chorazin! woe to thee Bethsaida! for if the works of power which have taken place in you, had taken place in Tyre and Sidon, they had long ago repented in sackcloth and ashes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say to you, that it shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon in judgment-day than for you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, Capernaum, who hast been raised up to heaven, shalt be brought down even to hades. For if the works of power which have taken place in thee, had taken place in Sodom, it had remained until this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say to you, that it shall be more tolerable for [the] land of Sodom in judgment-day than for thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At that time, Jesus answering said, I praise thee, Father, Lord of the heaven and of the earth, that thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them to babes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, Father, for thus has it been well-pleasing in thy sight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All things have been delivered to me by my Father, and no one knows the Son but the Father, nor does any one know the Father, but the Son, and he to whom the Son may be pleased to reveal [him].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Come to me, all ye who labour and are burdened, and I will give you rest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me; for I am meek and lowly in heart; and ye shall find rest to your souls;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At that time Jesus went on the sabbath through the cornfields; and his disciples were hungry, and began to pluck the ears and to eat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the Pharisees, seeing [it], said to him, Behold, thy disciples are doing what is not lawful to do on sabbath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he said to them, Have ye not read what David did when he was hungry, and they that were with him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How he entered into the house ofGod, and ate the shewbread, which it was not lawful for him to eat, nor for those with him, but for the priests only?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or have ye not read in the law that on the sabbaths the priests in the temple profane the sabbath, and are blameless?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say unto you, that there is here what is greater than the temple.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if ye had known what is: I will have mercy and not sacrifice, ye would not have condemned the guiltless.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the Son of man is Lord of the sabbath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, going away from thence, he came into their synagogue.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, there was a man having his hand withered. And they asked him, saying, Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath? that they might accuse him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he said to them, What man shall there be of you who has one sheep, and if this fall into a pit on the sabbath, will not lay hold of it and raise [it] up?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How much better then is a man than a sheep! So that it is lawful to do well on the sabbath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he says to the man, Stretch out thy hand. And he stretched [it] out, and it was restored sound as the other.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the Pharisees, having gone out, took counsel against him, how they might destroy him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus knowing [it], withdrew thence, and great crowds followed him; and he healed them all:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and charged them strictly that they should not make him publicly known:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that that might be fulfilled which was spoken through Esaias the prophet, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold my servant, whom I have chosen, my beloved, in whom my soul has found its delight. I will put my Spirit upon him, and he shall shew forth judgment to the nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall not strive or cry out, nor shall any one hear his voice in the streets;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a bruised reed shall he not break, and smoking flax shall he not quench, until he bring forth judgment unto victory;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and on his name shall [the] nations hope.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then was brought to him one possessed by a demon, blind and dumb, and he healed him, so that the dumb [man] spake and saw.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the crowds were amazed and said, Is this [man] the Son of David?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the Pharisees, having heard [it], said, This [man] does not cast out demons, but by Beelzebub, prince of demons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he, knowing their thoughts, said to them, Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation, and every city or house divided against itself will not subsist.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if Satan casts out Satan, he is divided against himself; how then shall his kingdom subsist?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if I cast out demons by Beelzebub, your sons, by whom do they cast [them] out? For this reason they shall be your judges.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if I by [the] Spirit ofGod cast out demons, then indeed the kingdom ofGod is come upon you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or how can any one enter into the house of the strong [man] and plunder his goods, unless first he bind the strong [man]? and then he will plunder his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that is not with me is against me, and he that gathers not with me scatters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this reason I say unto you, Every sin and injurious speaking shall be forgiven to men, but speaking injuriously of the Spirit shall not be forgiven to men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And whosoever shall have spoken a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him; but whosoever shall speak against the Holy Spirit, it shall not be forgiven him, neither in this age nor in the coming [one].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Either make the tree good, and its fruit good; or make the tree corrupt, and its fruit corrupt. For from the fruit the tree is known.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Offspring of vipers! how can ye speak good things, being wicked? For of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The good man out of the good treasure brings forth good things; and the wicked man out of the wicked treasure brings forth wicked things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say unto you, that every idle word which men shall say, they shall render an account of it in judgment-day:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for by thy words thou shalt be justified, and by thy words thou shalt be condemned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then answered him some of the scribes and Pharisees, saying, Teacher, we desire to see a sign from thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he, answering, said to them, A wicked and adulterous generation seeks after a sign, and a sign shall not be given to it save the sign of Jonas the prophet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For even as Jonas was in the belly of the great fish three days and three nights, thus shall the Son of man be in the heart of the earth three days and three nights.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ninevites shall stand up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for they repented at the preaching of Jonas; and behold, more than Jonas [is] here.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A queen of [the] south shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it; for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and behold, more than Solomon [is] here.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when the unclean spirit has gone out of the man, he goes through dry places, seeking rest, and does not find [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he says, I will return to my house whence I came out; and having come, he finds [it] unoccupied, swept, and adorned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he goes and takes with himself seven other spirits worse than himself, and entering in, they dwell there; and the last condition of that man becomes worse than the first. Thus shall it be to this wicked generation also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But while he was yet speaking to the crowds, behold, his mother and his brethren stood without, seeking to speak to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then one said unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren are standing without, seeking to speak to thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering said to him that spoke to him, Who is my mother, and who are my brethren?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, stretching out his hand to his disciples, he said, Behold my mother and my brethren;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for whosoever shall do the will of my Father who is in [the] heavens, he is my brother, and sister, and mother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And that [same] day Jesus went out from the house and sat down by the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And great crowds were gathered together to him, so that going on board ship himself he sat down, and the whole crowd stood on the shore.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spoke to them many things in parables, saying, Behold, the sower went out to sow:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and as he sowed, some [grains] fell along the way, and the birds came and devoured them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and others fell upon the rocky places where they had not much earth, and immediately they sprang up out of [the ground] because of not having [any] depth of earth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but when the sun rose they were burned up, and because of not having [any] root were dried up;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and others fell upon the thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and others fell upon the good ground, and produced fruit, one a hundred, one sixty, and one thirty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that has ears, let him hear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the disciples came up and said to him, Why speakest thou to them in parables?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he answering said to them, Because to you it is given to know the mysteries of the kingdom of the heavens, but to them it is not given;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for whoever has, to him shall be given, and he shall be caused to be in abundance; but he who has not, even what he has shall be taken away from him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this cause I speak to them in parables, because seeing they do not see, and hearing they do not hear nor understand;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and in them is filled up the prophecy of Esaias, which says, Hearing ye shall hear and shall not understand, and beholding ye shall behold and not see;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for the heart of this people has grown fat, and they have heard heavily with their ears, and they have closed their eyes as asleep, lest they should see with the eyes, and hear with the ears, and understand with the heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But blessed are your eyes because they see, and your ears because they hear;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for verily I say unto you, that many prophets and righteous [men] have desired to see the things which ye behold and did not see [them], and to hear the things which ye hear and did not hear [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye, therefore, hear the parable of the sower.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From every one who hears the word of the kingdom and does not understand [it], the wicked one comes and catches away what was sown in his heart: this is he that is sown by the wayside.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he that is sown on the rocky places — this is he who hears the word and immediately receives it with joy,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but has no root in himself, but is for a time only; and when tribulation or persecution happens on account of the word, he is immediately offended.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he that is sown among the thorns — this is he who hears the word, and the anxious care of this life, and the deceit of riches choke the word, and he becomes unfruitful.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he that is sown upon the good ground — this is he who hears and understands the word, who bears fruit also, and produces, one a hundred, one sixty, and one thirty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Another parable set he before them, saying, The kingdom of the heavens has become like a man sowing good seed in his field;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but while men slept, his enemy came and sowed darnel amongst the wheat, and went away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when the blade shot up and produced fruit, then appeared the darnel also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the bondmen of the householder came up and said to him, Sir, hast thou not sown good seed in thy field? whence then has it darnel?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, A man [that is] an enemy has done this. And the bondmen said to him, Wilt thou then that we should go and gather it [up]?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he said, No; lest [in] gathering the darnel ye should root up the wheat with it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Suffer both to grow together unto the harvest, and in time of the harvest I will say to the harvestmen, Gather first the darnel, and bind it into bundles to burn it; but the wheat bring together into my granary.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Another parable set he before them, saying, The kingdom of the heavens is like a grain of mustard [seed] which a man took and sowed in his field;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which is less indeed than all seeds, but when it is grown is greater than herbs, and becomes a tree, so that the birds of heaven come and roost in its branches.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He spoke another parable to them: The kingdom of the heavens is like leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal until it had been all leavened.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All these things Jesus spoke to the crowds in parables, and without a parable he did not speak to them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so that that should be fulfilled which was spoken through the prophet, saying, I will open my mouth in parables; I will utter things hidden from [the] world's foundation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then, having dismissed the crowds, he went into the house; and his disciples came to him, saying, Expound to us the parable of the darnel of the field.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering said, He that sows the good seed is the Son of man,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the field is the world; and the good seed, these are the sons of the kingdom, but the darnel are the sons of the evil [one];

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the enemy who has sowed it is the devil; and the harvest is [the] completion of [the] age, and the harvestmen are angels.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As then the darnel is gathered and is burned in the fire, thus it shall be in the completion of the age.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Son of man shall send his angels, and they shall gather out of his kingdom all offences, and those that practise lawlessness;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they shall cast them into the furnace of fire; there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the righteous shall shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father. He that has ears, let him hear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The kingdom of the heavens is like a treasure hid in the field, which a man having found has hid, and for the joy of it goes and sells all whatever he has, and buys that field.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Again, the kingdom of the heavens is like a merchant seeking beautiful pearls;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having found one pearl of great value, he went and sold all whatever he had and bought it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Again, the kingdom of the heavens is like a seine which has been cast into the sea, and which has gathered together of every kind,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which, when it has been filled, having drawn up on the shore and sat down, they gathered the good into vessels and cast the worthless out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus shall it be in the completion of the age: the angels shall go forth and sever the wicked from the midst of the just,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and shall cast them into the furnace of fire; there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to them, Have ye understood all these things? They say to him, Yea, [Lord].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, For this reason every scribe discipled to the kingdom of the heavens is like a man [that is] a householder who brings out of his treasure things new and old.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Jesus had finished these parables he withdrew thence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having come into his own country, he taught them in their synagogue, so that they were astonished, and said, Whence has this [man] this wisdom and these works of power?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is not this the son of the carpenter? Is not his mother called Mary, and his brethren James, and Joseph, and Simon, and Judas?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his sisters, are they not all with us? Whence then has this [man] all these things?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were offended in him. And Jesus said to them, A prophet is not without honour, unless in his country and in his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he did not there many works of power, because of their unbelief.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At that time Herod the tetrarch heard of the fame of Jesus,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said to his servants, This is John the baptist: he is risen from the dead, and because of this these works of power display their force in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Herod had seized John, and had bound him and put him in prison on account of Herodias the wife of Philip his brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For John said to him, It is not lawful for thee to have her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [while] desiring to kill him, he feared the crowd, because they held him for a prophet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when Herod's birthday was celebrated, the daughter of Herodias danced before them, and pleased Herod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: whereupon he promised with oath to give her whatsoever she should ask.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But she, being set on by her mother, says, Give me here upon a dish the head of John the baptist.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king was grieved; but on account of the oaths, and those lying at table with [him], he commanded [it] to be given.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he sent and beheaded John in the prison;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his head was brought upon a dish, and was given to the damsel, and she carried [it] to her mother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his disciples came and took the body and buried it, and came and brought word to Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, having heard it, went away thence by ship to a desert place apart. And the crowds having heard [of it] followed him on foot from the cities.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And going out he saw a great crowd, and was moved with compassion about them, and healed their infirm.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when even was come, his disciples came to him saying, The place is desert, and [much of] the [day] time already gone by; dismiss the crowds, that they may go into the villages and buy food for themselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said to them, They have no need to go: give ye them to eat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they say to him, We have not here save five loaves and two fishes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Bring them here to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having commanded the crowds to recline upon the grass, having taken the five loaves and the two fishes, he looked up to heaven, and blessed: and having broken the loaves, he gave [them] to the disciples, and the disciples [gave them] to the crowds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all ate and were filled, and they took up what was over and above of fragments twelve hand-baskets full.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But those that had eaten were about five thousand men, besides women and children.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately he compelled the disciples to go on board ship, and to go on before him to the other side, until he should have dismissed the crowds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having dismissed the crowds, he went up into the mountain apart to pray. And when even was come, he was alone there,

translations.name: Darby Bible
