translations.text: but the ship was already in the middle of the sea tossed by the waves, for the wind was contrary.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But in the fourth watch of the night he went off to them, walking on the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the disciples, seeing him walking on the sea, were troubled, saying, It is an apparition. And they cried out through fear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus immediately spoke to them, saying, Take courage; it is I: be not afraid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter answering him said, Lord, if it be thou, command me to come to thee upon the waters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Come. And Peter, having descended from the ship, walked upon the waters to go to Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But seeing the wind strong he was afraid; and beginning to sink he cried out, saying, Lord, save me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately Jesus stretched out his hand and caught hold of him, and says to him, O thou of little faith, why didst thou doubt?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they had gone up into the ship, the wind fell.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But those in the ship came and did homage to him, saying, Truly thou artGod's Son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having crossed over they came to the land of Gennesaret.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the men of that place recognised him, they sent to that whole country around, and they brought to him all that were ill,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and besought him that they might only touch the hem of his garment; and as many as touched were made thoroughly well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the scribes and Pharisees from Jerusalem come up to Jesus, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why do thy disciples transgress what has been delivered by the ancients? for they do not wash their hands when they eat bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering said to them, Why do ye also transgress the commandment ofGod on account of your traditional teaching?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ForGod commanded saying, Honour father and mother; and, He that speaks ill of father or mother, let him die the death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ye say, Whosoever shall say to his father or mother, It is a gift, whatsoever [it be] by which [received] from me thou wouldest be profited:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he shall in no wise honour his father or his mother; and ye have made void the commandment ofGod on account of your traditional teaching.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hypocrites! well has Esaias prophesied about you, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This people honour me with the lips, but their heart is far away from me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but in vain do they worship me, teaching [as] teachings commandments of men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having called to [him] the crowd, he said to them, Hear and understand:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Not what enters into the mouth defiles the man; but what goes forth out of the mouth, this defiles the man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then his disciples, coming up, said to him, Dost thou know that the Pharisees, having heard this word, have been offended?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering said, Every plant which my heavenly Father has not planted shall be rooted up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Leave them alone; they are blind leaders of blind: but if blind lead blind, both will fall into a ditch.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter answering said to him, Expound to us this parable.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he said, Are ye also still without intelligence?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do ye not yet apprehend, that everything that enters into the mouth finds its way into the belly, and is cast forth into the draught?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but the things which go forth out of the mouth come out of the heart, and those defile man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For out of the heart come forth evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, fornications, thefts, false witnessings, blasphemies;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: these are the things which defile man; but the eating with unwashen hands does not defile man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, going forth from thence, went away into the parts of Tyre and Sidon;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and lo, a Canaanitish woman, coming out from those borders, cried [to him] saying, Have pity on me, Lord, Son of David; my daughter is miserably possessed by a demon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he did not answer her a word. And his disciples came to [him] and asked him, saying, Dismiss her, for she cries after us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering said, I have not been sent save to the lost sheep of Israel's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But she came and did him homage, saying, Lord, help me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering said, It is not well to take the bread of the children and cast it to the dogs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But she said, Yea, Lord; for even the dogs eat of the crumbs which fall from the table of their masters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Jesus answering said to her, O woman, thy faith [is] great. Be it to thee as thou desirest. And her daughter was healed from that hour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, going away from thence, came towards the sea of Galilee, and he went up into the mountain and sat down there;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and great crowds came to him, having with them lame, blind, dumb, crippled, and many others, and they cast them at his feet, and he healed them:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so that the crowds wondered, seeing dumb speaking, crippled sound, lame walking, and blind seeing; and they glorified theGod of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus, having called his disciples to [him], said, I have compassion on the crowd, because they have stayed with me already three days and they have not anything they can eat, and I would not send them away fasting lest they should faint on the way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his disciples say to him, Whence should we have so many loaves in [the] wilderness as to satisfy so great a crowd?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus says to them, How many loaves have ye? But they said, Seven, and a few small fishes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he commanded the crowds to lie down on the ground;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having taken the seven loaves and the fishes, having given thanks, he broke [them] and gave [them] to his disciples, and the disciples to the crowd.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all ate and were filled; and they took up what was over and above of the fragments seven baskets full;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but they that ate were four thousand men, besides women and children.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, having dismissed the crowds, he went on board ship and came to the borders of Magadan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Pharisees and Sadducees, coming to [him], asked him, tempting [him], to shew them a sign out of heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering said to them, When evening is come, ye say, Fine weather, for the sky is red;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and in the morning, A storm to-day, for the sky is red [and] lowering; ye know [how] to discern the face of the sky, but ye cannot the signs of the times.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A wicked and adulterous generation seeks after a sign, and a sign shall not be given to it save the sign of Jonas. And he left them and went away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when his disciples were come to the other side, they had forgotten to take bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to them, See and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, Because we have taken no bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus knowing [it], said, Why reason ye among yourselves, O ye of little faith, because ye have taken no bread?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do ye not yet understand nor remember the five loaves of the five thousand, and how many hand-baskets ye took [up]?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: nor the seven loaves of the four thousand, and how many baskets ye took [up]?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How do ye not understand that [it was] not concerning bread I said to you, Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then they comprehended that he did not speak of being beware of the leaven of bread, but of the doctrine of the Pharisees and Sadducees.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when Jesus was come into the parts of Caesarea-Philippi, he demanded of his disciples, saying, Who do men say that I the Son of man am?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, Some, John the baptist; and others, Elias; and others again, Jeremias or one of the prophets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He says to them, But ye, who do ye say that I am?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Simon Peter answering said, Thou art the Christ, the Son of the livingGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to him, Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona, for flesh and blood has not revealed [it] to thee, but my Father who is in the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I also, I say unto thee that thou art Peter, and on this rock I will build my assembly, and hades' gates shall not prevail against it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will give to thee the keys of the kingdom of the heavens; and whatsoever thou mayest bind upon the earth shall be bound in the heavens; and whatsoever thou mayest loose on the earth shall be loosed in the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he enjoined on his disciples that they should say to no man that he was the Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From that time Jesus began to shew to his disciples that he must go away to Jerusalem, and suffer many things from the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and the third day be raised.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter taking him to [him] began to rebuke him, saying, [God] be favourable to thee, Lord; this shall in no wise be unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But turning round, he said to Peter, Get away behind me, Satan; thou art an offence to me, for thy mind is not on the things that are ofGod, but on the things that are of men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Jesus said to his disciples, If any one desires to come after me, let him deny himself and take up his cross and follow me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For whosoever shall desire to save his life shall lose it; but whosoever shall lose his life for my sake shall find it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For what does a man profit, if he should gain the whole world and suffer the loss of his soul? or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the Son of man is about to come in the glory of his Father with his angels, and then he will render to each according to his doings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily I say unto you, There are some of those standing here that shall not taste of death at all until they shall have seen the Son of man coming in his kingdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after six days Jesus takes with [him] Peter, and James, and John his brother, and brings them up into a high mountain apart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he was transfigured before them. And his face shone as the sun, and his garments became white as the light;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and lo, Moses and Elias appeared to them talking with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter answering said to Jesus, Lord, it is good we should be here. If thou wilt, let us make here three tabernacles: for thee one, and for Moses one, and one for Elias.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: While he was still speaking, behold, a bright cloud overshadowed them, and lo, a voice out of the cloud, saying, This is my beloved Son, in whom I have found my delight: hear him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the disciples hearing [it] fell upon their faces, and were greatly terrified.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus coming to [them] touched them, and said, Rise up, and be not terrified.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And lifting up their eyes, they saw no one but Jesus alone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they descended from the mountain, Jesus charged them, saying, Tell the vision to no one, until the Son of man be risen up from among [the] dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [his] disciples demanded of him saying, Why then say the scribes that Elias must first have come?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he answering said to them, Elias indeed comes first and will restore all things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say unto you that Elias has already come, and they have not known him, but have done unto him whatever they would. Thus also the Son of man is about to suffer from them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the disciples understood that he spoke to them of John the baptist.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they came to the crowd, a man came to him, falling on his knees before him, and saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lord, have mercy on my son, for he is lunatic, and suffers sorely; for often he falls into the fire and often into the water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I brought him to thy disciples, and they were not able to heal him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said, O unbelieving and perverted generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I bear with you? Bring him here to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus rebuked him, and the demon went out from him, and the boy was healed from that hour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the disciples, coming to Jesus apart, said [to him], Why were not we able to cast him out?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he says to them, Because of your unbelief; for verily I say unto you, If ye have faith as a grain of mustard [seed], ye shall say to this mountain, Be transported hence there, and it shall transport itself; and nothing shall be impossible to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But this kind does not go out but by prayer and fasting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And while they abode in Galilee, Jesus said to them, The Son of man is about to be delivered up into [the] hands of men,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they shall kill him; and the third day he shall be raised up. And they were greatly grieved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they came to Capernaum, those who received the didrachmas came to Peter and said, Does your teacher not pay the didrachmas?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He says, Yes. And when he came into the house, Jesus anticipated him, saying, What dost thou think, Simon? the kings of the earth, from whom do they receive custom or tribute? from their own sons or from strangers?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Peter says to him, From strangers. Jesus said to him, Then are the sons free.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But that we may not be an offence to them, go to the sea and cast a hook, and take the first fish that comes up, and when thou hast opened its mouth thou wilt find a stater; take that and give it to them for me and thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In that hour the disciples came to Jesus saying, Who then is greatest in the kingdom of the heavens?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus having called a little child to [him], set it in their midst,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said, Verily I say to you, Unless ye are converted and become as little children, ye will not at all enter into the kingdom of the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whoever therefore shall humble himself as this little child, he is the greatest in the kingdom of the heavens;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and whosoever shall receive one such little child in my name, receives me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But whosoever shall offend one of these little ones who believe in me, it were profitable for him that a great millstone had been hanged upon his neck and he be sunk in the depths of the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe to the world because of offences! For it must needs be that offences come; yet woe to that man by whom the offence comes!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if thy hand or thy foot offend thee, cut it off and cast [it] from thee; it is good for thee to enter into life lame or maimed, [rather] than having two hands or two feet to be cast into eternal fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out and cast [it] from thee; it is good for thee to enter into life one-eyed, [rather] than having two eyes to be cast into the hell of fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: See that ye do not despise one of these little ones; for I say unto you that their angels in [the] heavens continually behold the face of my Father who is in [the] heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the Son of man has come to save that which was lost.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What think ye? If a certain man should have a hundred sheep, and one of them be gone astray, does he not, leaving the ninety and nine on the mountains, go and seek the one that has gone astray?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if it should come to pass that he find it, verily I say unto you, he rejoices more because of it than because of the ninety and nine not gone astray.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So it is not the will of your Father who is in [the] heavens that one of these little ones should perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if thy brother sin against thee, go, reprove him between thee and him alone. If he hear thee, thou hast gained thy brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if he do not hear [thee], take with thee one or two besides, that every matter may stand upon the word of two witnesses or of three.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if he will not listen to them, tell it to the assembly; and if also he will not listen to the assembly, let him be to thee as one of the nations and a tax-gatherer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily I say to you, Whatsoever ye shall bind on the earth shall be bound in heaven, and whatsoever ye shall loose on the earth shall be loosed in heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Again I say to you, that if two of you shall agree on the earth concerning any matter, whatsoever it may be that they shall ask, it shall come to them from my Father who is in [the] heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For where two or three are gathered together unto my name, there am I in the midst of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Peter came to him and said, Lord, how often shall my brother sin against me and I forgive him? until seven times?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to him, I say not to thee until seven times, but until seventy times seven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this cause the kingdom of the heavens has become like a king who would reckon with his bondmen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having begun to reckon, one debtor of ten thousand talents was brought to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he not having anything to pay, [his] lord commanded him to be sold, and his wife, and his children, and everything that he had, and that payment should be made.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The bondman therefore falling down did him homage, saying, Lord, have patience with me and I will pay thee all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the lord of that bondman, being moved with compassion, loosed him and forgave him the loan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But that bondman having gone out, found one of his fellow-bondmen who owed him a hundred denarii. And having seized him, he throttled him, saying, Pay [me] if thou owest anything.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His fellow-bondman therefore, having fallen down [at his feet], besought him, saying, Have patience with me, and I will pay thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he would not, but went away and cast him into prison, until he should pay what was owing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But his fellow-bondmen, having seen what had taken place, were greatly grieved, and went and recounted to their lord all that had taken place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then his lord, having called him to [him], says to him, Wicked bondman! I forgave thee all that debt because thou besoughtest me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: shouldest not thou also have had compassion on thy fellow-bondman, as I also had compassion on thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his lord being angry delivered him to the tormentors till he paid all that was owing to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus also my heavenly Father shall do to you if ye forgive not from your hearts every one his brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Jesus had finished these words, he withdrew from Galilee, and came to the coasts of Judaea beyond the Jordan;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and great crowds followed him, and he healed them there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Pharisees came to him tempting him, and saying, Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife for every cause?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering said [to them], Have ye not read that he who made [them], from the beginning made them male and female,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said, On account of this a man shall leave father and mother, and shall be united to his wife, and the two shall be one flesh?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so that they are no longer two, but one flesh. What thereforeGod has joined together, let not man separate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They say to him, Why then did Moses command to give a letter of divorce and to send [her] away?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He says to them, Moses, in view of your hardheartedness, allowed you to put away your wives; but from the beginning it was not thus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say unto you, that whosoever shall put away his wife, not for fornication, and shall marry another, commits adultery; and he who marries one put away commits adultery.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His disciples say to him, If the case of the man be so with his wife, it is not good to marry.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, All cannot receive this word, but those to whom it has been given;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for there are eunuchs which have been born thus from [their] mother's womb; and there are eunuchs who have been made eunuchs of men; and there are eunuchs who have made eunuchs of themselves for the sake of the kingdom of the heavens. He that is able to receive [it], let him receive [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then there were brought to him little children that he might lay his hands on them and pray; but the disciples rebuked them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said, Suffer little children, and do not hinder them from coming to me; for the kingdom of the heavens is of such:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having laid his hands upon them, he departed thence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And lo, one coming up said to him, Teacher, what good thing shall I do that I may have life eternal?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, What askest thou me concerning goodness? one is good. But if thou wouldest enter into life, keep the commandments.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He says to him, Which? And Jesus said, Thou shalt not kill, Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt not steal, Thou shalt not bear false witness,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Honour thy father and thy mother, and Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The young man says to him, All these have I kept; what lack I yet?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to him, If thou wouldest be perfect, go, sell what thou hast and give to [the] poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the young man, having heard the word, went away grieved, for he had large possessions.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to his disciples, Verily I say unto you, A rich man shall with difficulty enter into the kingdom of the heavens;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and again I say unto you, It is easier for a camel to enter a needle's eye than a rich man into the kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the disciples heard [it] they were exceedingly astonished, saying, Who then can be saved?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus, looking on [them], said to them, With men this is impossible; but withGod all things are possible.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Peter answering said to him, Behold, we have left all things and have followed thee; what then shall happen to us?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to them, Verily I say unto you, That ye who have followed me, in the regeneration when the Son of man shall sit down upon his throne of glory, ye also shall sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And every one who has left houses, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my name's sake, shall receive a hundredfold, and shall inherit life eternal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But many first shall be last, and last first.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the kingdom of the heavens is like a householder who went out with the early morn to hire workmen for his vineyard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having agreed with the workmen for a denarius the day, he sent them into his vineyard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having gone out about [the] third hour, he saw others standing in the market-place idle;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to them he said, Go also ye into the vineyard, and whatsoever may be just I will give you. And they went their way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Again, having gone out about the sixth and ninth hour, he did likewise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But about the eleventh [hour], having gone out, he found others standing, and says to them, Why stand ye here all the day idle?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They say to him, Because no man has hired us. He says to them, Go also ye into the vineyard [and whatsoever may be just ye shall receive].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when the evening was come, the lord of the vineyard says to his steward, Call the workmen and pay [them] their wages, beginning from the last even to the first.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they [who came to work] about the eleventh hour came, they received each a denarius.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the first came, they supposed that they would receive more, and they received also themselves each a denarius.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on receiving it they murmured against the master of the house,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, These last have worked one hour, and thou hast made them equal to us, who have borne the burden of the day and the heat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering said to one of them, [My] friend, I do not wrong thee. Didst thou not agree with me for a denarius?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take what is thine and go. But it is my will to give to this last even as to thee:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: is it not lawful for me to do what I will in my own affairs? Is thine eye evil because I am good?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus shall the last be first, and the first last; for many are called ones, but few chosen ones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, going up to Jerusalem, took the twelve disciples with [him] apart in the way, and said to them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold we go up to Jerusalem, and the Son of man will be delivered up to the chief priests and scribes, and they will condemn him to death;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they will deliver him up to the nations to mock and to scourge and to crucify, and the third day he shall rise again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then came to him the mother of the sons of Zebedee, with her sons, doing homage, and asking something of him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to her, What wilt thou? She says to him, Speak [the word] that these my two sons may sit, one on thy right hand and one on thy left in thy kingdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said, Ye know not what ye ask. Can ye drink the cup which I am about to drink? They say to him, We are able.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [And] he says to them, Ye shall drink indeed my cup, but to sit on my right hand and on [my] left, is not mine to give, but to those for whom it is prepared of my Father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the ten, having heard [of it], were indignant about the two brothers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus having called them to [him], said, Ye know that the rulers of the nations exercise lordship over them, and the great exercise authority over them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It shall not be thus amongst you, but whosoever will be great among you, shall be your servant;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and whosoever will be first among you, let him be your bondman;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: as indeed the Son of man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give his life a ransom for many.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they went out from Jericho a great crowd followed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And lo, two blind men, sitting by the wayside, having heard that Jesus was passing by, cried out saying, Have mercy on us, Lord, Son of David.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the crowd rebuked them, that they might be silent. But they cried out the more, saying, Have mercy on us, Lord, Son of David.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, having stopped, called them and said, What will ye that I shall do to you?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They say to him, Lord, that our eyes may be opened.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, moved with compassion, touched their eyes; and immediately their eyes had sight restored to them, and they followed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they drew near to Jerusalem and came to Bethphage, at the mount of Olives, then Jesus sent two disciples,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying to them, Go into the village over against you, and immediately ye will find an ass tied, and a colt with it; loose [them] and lead [them] to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if any one say anything to you, ye shall say, The Lord has need of them, and straightway he will send them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But all this came to pass, that that might be fulfilled which was spoken through the prophet, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Say to the daughter of Zion, Behold thy King cometh to thee, meek, and mounted upon an ass, and upon a colt the foal of an ass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the disciples, having gone and done as Jesus had ordered them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: brought the ass and the colt and put their garments upon them, and he sat on them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But a very great crowd strewed their own garments on the way, and others kept cutting down branches from the trees and strewing them on the way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the crowds who went before him and who followed cried, saying, Hosanna to the Son of David; blessed [be] he who comes in the name of [the] Lord; hosanna in the highest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he entered into Jerusalem, the whole city was moved, saying, Who is this?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the crowds said, This is Jesus the prophet who is from Nazareth of Galilee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus entered into the temple [ofGod], and cast out all that sold and bought in the temple, and overthrew the tables of the money-changers and the seats of those that sold the doves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he says to them, It is written, My house shall be called a house of prayer, but ye have made it a den of robbers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And blind and lame came to him in the temple, and he healed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the chief priests and the scribes saw the wonders which he wrought, and the children crying in the temple and saying, Hosanna to the Son of David, they were indignant,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said to him, Hearest thou what these say? And Jesus says to them, Yea; have ye never read, Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings thou hast perfected praise?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And leaving them he went forth out of the city to Bethany, and there he passed the night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But early in the morning, as he came back into the city, he hungered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And seeing one fig-tree in the way, he came to it and found on it nothing but leaves only. And he says to it, Let there be never more fruit of thee for ever. And the fig-tree was immediately dried up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the disciples saw [it], they wondered, saying, How immediately is the fig-tree dried up!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, Verily I say unto you, If ye have faith, and do not doubt, not only shall ye do what [is done] to the fig-tree, but even if ye should say to this mountain, Be thou taken away and be thou cast into the sea, it shall come to pass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all things whatsoever ye shall ask in prayer, believing, ye shall receive.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he came into the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people came to him [as he was] teaching, saying, By what authority doest thou these things? and who gave thee this authority?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, I also will ask you one thing, which if ye tell me, I also will tell you by what authority I do these things:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The baptism of John, whence was it? of heaven or of men? And they reasoned among themselves, saying, If we should say, Of heaven, he will say to us, Why then have ye not believed him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but if we should say, Of men, we fear the crowd, for all hold John for a prophet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And answering Jesus they said, We do not know. He also said to them, Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But what think ye? A man had two children, and coming to the first he said, Child, go to-day, work in [my] vineyard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he answering said, I will not; but afterwards repenting himself he went.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And coming to the second he said likewise; and he answering said, I [go], sir, and went not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Which of the two did the will of the father? They say [to him], The first. Jesus says to them, Verily I say unto you that the tax-gatherers and the harlots go into the kingdom ofGod before you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For John came to you in the way of righteousness, and ye believed him not; but the tax-gatherers and the harlots believed him; but ye when ye saw [it] repented not yourselves afterwards to believe him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear another parable: There was a householder who planted a vineyard, and made a fence round it, and dug a winepress in it, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen, and left the country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when the time of fruit drew near, he sent his bondmen to the husbandmen to receive his fruits.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the husbandmen took his bondmen, and beat one, killed another, and stoned another.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Again he sent other bondmen more than the first, and they did to them in like manner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And at last he sent to them his son, saying, They will have respect for my son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the husbandmen, seeing the son, said among themselves, This is the heir; come, let us kill him and possess his inheritance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took him, and cast him forth out of the vineyard, and killed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When therefore the lord of the vineyard comes, what shall he do to those husbandmen?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They say to him, He will miserably destroy those evil [men], and let out the vineyard to other husbandmen, who shall render him the fruits in their seasons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to them, Have ye never read in the scriptures, The stone which they that builded rejected, this has become the corner-stone: this is of [the] Lord, and it is wonderful in our eyes?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore I say to you, that the kingdom ofGod shall be taken from you and shall be given to a nation producing the fruits of it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he that falls on this stone shall be broken, but on whomsoever it shall fall, it shall grind him to powder.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the chief priests and the Pharisees, having heard his parables, knew that he spoke about them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And seeking to lay hold of him, they were afraid of the crowds, because they held him for a prophet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering spoke to them again in parables, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The kingdom of the heavens has become like a king who made a wedding feast for his son,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and sent his bondmen to call the persons invited to the wedding feast, and they would not come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Again he sent other bondmen, saying, Say to the persons invited, Behold, I have prepared my dinner; my oxen and my fatted beasts are killed, and all things ready; come to the wedding feast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they made light of it, and went, one to his own land, and another to his commerce.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the rest, laying hold of his bondmen, ill-treated and slew [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [when] the king [heard of it he] was wroth, and having sent his forces, destroyed those murderers and burned their city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he says to his bondmen, The wedding feast is ready, but those invited were not worthy;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: go therefore into the thoroughfares of the highways, and as many as ye shall find invite to the wedding feast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those bondmen went out into the highways, and brought together all as many as they found, both evil and good; and the wedding feast was furnished with guests.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king, having gone in to see the guests, beheld there a man not clothed with a wedding garment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he says to him, [My] friend, how camest thou in here not having on a wedding garment? But he was speechless.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said the king to the servants, Bind him feet and hands, and take him away, and cast him out into the outer darkness: there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For many are called ones, but few chosen ones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then went the Pharisees and held a council how they might ensnare him in speaking.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they send out to him their disciples with the Herodians, saying, Teacher, we know that thou art true and teachest the way ofGod in truth, and carest not for any one, for thou regardest not men's person;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: tell us therefore what thou thinkest: Is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar, or not?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus, knowing their wickedness, said, Why tempt ye me, hypocrites?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shew me the money of the tribute. And they presented to him a denarius.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he says to them, Whose [is] this image and superscription?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They say to him, Caesar's. Then he says to them, Pay then what is Caesar's to Caesar, and what isGod's toGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they heard [him], they wondered, and left him, and went away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On that day came to him Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection; and they demanded of him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, Teacher, Moses said, If any one die, not having children, his brother shall marry his wife and shall raise up seed to his brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now there were with us seven brethren; and the first having married died, and not having seed, left his wife to his brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In like manner also the second and the third, unto the seven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And last of all the woman also died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the resurrection therefore of which of the seven shall she be wife, for all had her?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, Ye err, not knowing the scriptures nor the power ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For in the resurrection they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are as angels ofGod in heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But concerning the resurrection of the dead, have ye not read what was spoken to you byGod, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am theGod of Abraham, and theGod of Isaac, and theGod of Jacob?God is notGod of [the] dead, but of [the] living.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the crowds heard [it] they were astonished at his doctrine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the Pharisees, having heard that he had put the Sadducees to silence, were gathered together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And one of them, a lawyer, demanded, tempting him, and saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Teacher, which is the great commandment in the law?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, Thou shalt love [the] Lord thyGod with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is [the] great and first commandment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [the] second is like it, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On these two commandments the whole law and the prophets hang.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Pharisees being gathered together, Jesus demanded of them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, What think ye concerning the Christ? whose son is he? They say to him, David's.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He says to them, How then does David in Spirit call him Lord, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Lord said to my Lord, Sit on my right hand until I put thine enemies under thy feet?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If therefore David call him Lord, how is he his son?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And no one was able to answer him a word, nor did any one dare from that day to question him any more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Jesus spoke to the crowds and to his disciples,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, The scribes and the Pharisees have set themselves down in Moses' seat:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: all things therefore, whatever they may tell you, do and keep. But do not after their works, for they say and do not,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but bind burdens heavy and hard to bear, and lay them on the shoulders of men, but will not move them with their finger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all their works they do to be seen of men: for they make broad their phylacteries and enlarge the borders [of their garments],

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and love the chief place in feasts and the first seats in the synagogues,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and salutations in the market-places, and to be called of men, Rabbi, Rabbi.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ye, be not ye called Rabbi; for one is your instructor, and all ye are brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And call not [any one] your father upon the earth; for one is your Father, he who is in the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Neither be called instructors, for one is your instructor, the Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the greatest of you shall be your servant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And whoever shall exalt himself shall be humbled, and whoever shall humble himself shall be exalted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, for ye shut up the kingdom of the heavens before men; for ye do not enter, nor do ye suffer those that are entering to go in.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, for ye compass the sea and the dry [land] to make one proselyte, and when he is become [such], ye make him twofold more [the] son of hell than yourselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe to you, blind guides, who say, Whosoever shall swear by the temple, it is nothing; but whosoever shall swear by the gold of the temple, he is a debtor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fools and blind, for which is greater, the gold, or the temple which sanctifies the gold?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, Whosoever shall swear by the altar, it is nothing; but whosoever shall swear by the gift that is upon it is a debtor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Fools and] blind ones, for which is greater, the gift, or the altar which sanctifies the gift?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He therefore that swears by the altar swears by it and by all things that are upon it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he that swears by the temple swears by it and by him that dwells in it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he that swears by heaven swears by the throne ofGod and by him that sits upon it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, for ye pay tithes of mint and anise and cummin, and ye have left aside the weightier matters of the law, judgment and mercy and faith: these ye ought to have done and not have left those aside.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blind guides, who strain out the gnat, but drink down the camel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, for ye make clean the outside of the cup and of the dish, but within they are full of rapine and intemperance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blind Pharisee, make clean first the inside of the cup and of the dish, that their outside also may become clean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, for ye are like whited sepulchres, which appear beautiful outwardly, but within are full of dead men's bones and all uncleanness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus also ye, outwardly ye appear righteous to men, but within are full of hypocrisy and lawlessness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, for ye build the sepulchres of the prophets and adorn the tombs of the just,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye say, If we had been in the days of our fathers we would not have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So that ye bear witness of yourselves that ye are sons of those who slew the prophets:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and ye, fill ye up the measure of your fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Serpents, offspring of vipers, how should ye escape the judgment of hell?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore, behold, I send unto you prophets, and wise men, and scribes; and [some] of them ye will kill and crucify, and [some] of them ye will scourge in your synagogues, and will persecute from city to city;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so that all righteous blood shed upon the earth should come upon you, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zacharias son of Barachias, whom ye slew between the temple and the altar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily I say unto you, All these things shall come upon this generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, [the city] that kills the prophets and stones those that are sent unto her, how often would I have gathered thy children as a hen gathers her chickens under her wings, and ye would not!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, your house is left unto you desolate;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for I say unto you, Ye shall in no wise see me henceforth until ye say, Blessed [be] he that comes in the name of [the] Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus went forth and went away from the temple, and his disciples came to [him] to point out to him the buildings of the temple.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he answering said to them, Do ye not see all these things? Verily I say to you, Not a stone shall be left here upon a stone which shall not be thrown down.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he was sitting upon the mount of Olives the disciples came to him privately, saying, Tell us, when shall these things be, and what is the sign of thy coming and [the] completion of the age?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, See that no one mislead you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For many shall come in my name, saying, I am the Christ, and they shall mislead many.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ye will hear of wars and rumours of wars. See that ye be not disturbed; for all [these things] must take place, but it is not yet the end.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For nation shall rise up against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; and there shall be famines and pestilences, and earthquakes in divers places.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But all these [are the] beginning of throes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shall they deliver you up to tribulation, and shall kill you; and ye will be hated of all the nations for my name's sake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And then will many be offended, and will deliver one another up, and hate one another;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and many false prophets shall arise and shall mislead many;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and because lawlessness shall prevail, the love of the most shall grow cold;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he that has endured to the end, he shall be saved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these glad tidings of the kingdom shall be preached in the whole habitable earth, for a witness to all the nations, and then shall come the end.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When therefore ye shall see the abomination of desolation, which is spoken of through Daniel the prophet, standing in [what is a] holy place, (he that reads let him understand,)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then let those who are in Judaea flee to the mountains;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: let not him that is on the house come down to take the things out of his house;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and let not him that is in the field turn back to take his garment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But woe to those that are with child, and those that give suck in those days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But pray that your flight may not be in winter time nor on sabbath:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for then shall there be great tribulation, such as has not been from [the] beginning of [the] world until now, nor ever shall be;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and if those days had not been cut short, no flesh had been saved; but on account of the elect those days shall be cut short.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then if any one say to you, Behold, here is the Christ, or here, believe [it] not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For there shall arise false Christs, and false prophets, and shall give great signs and wonders, so as to mislead, if possible, even the elect.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I have told you beforehand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If therefore they say to you, Behold, he is in the desert, go not forth; behold, [he is] in the inner chambers, do not believe [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For as the lightning goes forth from the east and shines to the west, so shall be the coming of the Son of man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [For] wherever the carcase is, there will be gathered the eagles.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But immediately after the tribulation of those days the sun shall be darkened, and the moon not give her light, and the stars shall fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens shall be shaken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven; and then shall all the tribes of the land lament, and they shall see the Son of man coming on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he shall send his angels with a great sound of trumpet, and they shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from [the one] extremity of [the] heavens to [the other] extremity of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But learn the parable from the fig-tree: When already its branch becomes tender and produces leaves, ye know that the summer is near.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus also ye, when ye see all these things, know that it is near, at the doors.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily I say to you, This generation will not have passed away until all these things shall have taken place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The heaven and the earth shall pass away, but my words shall in no wise pass away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But of that day and hour no one knows, not even the angels of the heavens, but [my] Father alone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as the days of Noe, so also shall be the coming of the Son of man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For as they were in the days which were before the flood, eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day on which Noe entered into the ark,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they knew not till the flood came and took all away; thus also shall be the coming of the Son of man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then two shall be in the field, one is taken and one is left;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: two [women] grinding at the mill, one is taken and one is left.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Watch therefore, for ye know not in what hour your Lord comes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But know this, that if the master of the house had known in what watch the thief was coming, he would have watched and not have suffered his house to be dug through [into].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore ye also, be ye ready, for in that hour that ye think not the Son of man comes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who then is the faithful and prudent bondman whom his lord has set over his household, to give them food in season?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed is that bondman whom his lord on coming shall find doing thus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily I say unto you, that he will set him over all his substance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if that evil bondman should say in his heart, My lord delays to come,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and begin to beat his fellow-bondmen, and eat and drink with the drunken;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the lord of that bondman shall come in a day when he does not expect it, and in an hour he knows not of,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and shall cut him in two and appoint his portion with the hypocrites: there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shall the kingdom of the heavens be made like to ten virgins that having taken their torches, went forth to meet the bridegroom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And five of them were prudent and five foolish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They that were foolish took their torches and did not take oil with them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but the prudent took oil in their vessels with their torches.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the bridegroom tarrying, they all grew heavy and slept.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But in [the] middle of [the] night there was a cry, Behold, the bridegroom; go forth to meet him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then all those virgins arose and trimmed their torches.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the foolish said to the prudent, Give us of your oil, for our torches are going out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the prudent answered saying, [We cannot,] lest it might not suffice for us and for you. Go rather to those that sell, and buy for yourselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as they went away to buy, the bridegroom came, and the [ones that were] ready went in with him to the wedding feast, and the door was shut.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Afterwards come also the rest of the virgins, saying, Lord, Lord, open to us;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he answering said, Verily I say unto you, I do not know you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Watch therefore, for ye know not the day nor the hour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For [it is] as [if] a man going away out of a country called his own bondmen and delivered to them his substance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And to one he gave five talents, to another two, and to another one; to each according to his particular ability, and immediately went away out of the country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he that had received the five talents went and trafficked with them, and made five other talents.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In like manner also he that [had received] the two, [he also] gained two others.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he that had received the one went and dug in the earth, and hid the money of his lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after a long time the lord of those bondmen comes and reckons with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he that had received the five talents came to [him] and brought five other talents, saying, [My] lord, thou deliveredst me five talents; behold, I have gained five other talents besides them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His lord said to him, Well, good and faithful bondman, thou wast faithful over a few things, I will set thee over many things: enter into the joy of thy lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he also that had received the two talents came to [him] and said, [My] lord, thou deliveredst me two talents; behold, I have gained two other talents besides them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His lord said to him, Well, good and faithful bondman, thou wast faithful over a few things, I will set thee over many things: enter thou into the joy of thy lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he also that had received the one talent coming to [him] said, [My] lord, I knew thee that thou art a hard man, reaping where thou hadst not sowed, and gathering from where thou hadst not scattered,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and being afraid I went away and hid thy talent in the earth; behold, thou hast that which is thine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his lord answering said to him, Wicked and slothful bondman, thou knewest that I reap where I had not sowed, and gather from where I had not scattered;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thou oughtest then to have put my money to the money-changers, and when I came I should have got what is mine with interest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take therefore the talent from him, and give it to him that has the ten talents:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for to every one that has shall be given, and he shall be in abundance; but from him that has not, that even which he has shall be taken from him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And cast out the useless bondman into the outer darkness; there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when the Son of man comes in his glory, and all the angels with him, then shall he sit down upon his throne of glory,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all the nations shall be gathered before him; and he shall separate them from one another, as the shepherd separates the sheep from the goats;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he will set the sheep on his right hand, and the goats on [his] left.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shall the King say to those on his right hand, Come, blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from [the] world's foundation:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for I hungered, and ye gave me to eat; I thirsted, and ye gave me to drink; I was a stranger, and ye took me in;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: naked, and ye clothed me; I was ill, and ye visited me; I was in prison, and ye came to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shall the righteous answer him saying, Lord, when saw we thee hungering, and nourished thee; or thirsting, and gave thee to drink?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and when saw we thee a stranger, and took thee in; or naked, and clothed thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and when saw we thee ill, or in prison, and came to thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the King answering shall say to them, Verily, I say to you, Inasmuch as ye have done it to one of the least of these my brethren, ye have done it to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shall he say also to those on the left, Go from me, cursed, into eternal fire, prepared for the devil and his angels:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for I hungered, and ye gave me not to eat; I thirsted, and ye gave me not to drink;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I was a stranger, and ye took me not in; naked, and ye did not clothe me; ill, and in prison, and ye did not visit me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shall they also answer saying, Lord, when saw we thee hungering, or thirsting, or a stranger, or naked, or ill, or in prison, and have not ministered to thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then shall he answer them saying, Verily I say to you, Inasmuch as ye have not done it to one of these least, neither have ye done it to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these shall go away into eternal punishment, and the righteous into life eternal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Jesus had finished all these sayings, he said to his disciples,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye know that after two days the passover takes place, and the Son of man is delivered up to be crucified.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the chief priests and the elders of the people were gathered together to the palace of the high priest who was called Caiaphas,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and took counsel together in order that they might seize Jesus by subtlety and kill him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but they said, Not in the feast, that there be not a tumult among the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus being in Bethany, in Simon the leper's house,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: a woman, having an alabaster flask of very precious ointment, came to him and poured it out upon his head as he lay at table.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the disciples seeing it became indignant, saying, To what end [was] this waste?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for this might have been sold for much and been given to the poor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus knowing [it] said to them, Why do ye trouble the woman? for she has wrought a good work toward me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ye have the poor always with you, but me ye have not always.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For in pouring out this ointment on my body, she has done it for my burying.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily I say to you, Wheresoever these glad tidings may be preached in the whole world, that also which this [woman] has done shall be spoken of for a memorial of her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then one of the twelve, he who was called Judas Iscariote, went to the chief priests

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said, What are ye willing to give me, and I will deliver him up to you? And they appointed to him thirty pieces of silver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And from that time he sought a good opportunity that he might deliver him up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now on the first [day] of [the feast of] unleavened bread, the disciples came to Jesus, saying, Where wilt thou that we prepare for thee to eat the passover?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Go into the city unto such a one, and say to him, The Teacher says, My time is near, I will keep the passover in thy house with my disciples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the disciples did as Jesus had directed them, and they prepared the passover.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the evening was come he lay down at table with the twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they were eating he said, Verily I say to you, that one of you shall deliver me up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And being exceedingly grieved they began to say to him, each of them, Is it I, Lord?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering said, He that dips his hand with me in the dish, he it is who shall deliver me up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Son of man goes indeed, according as it is written concerning him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of man is delivered up; it were good for that man if he had not been born.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judas, who delivered him up, answering said, Is it I, Rabbi? He says to him, Thou hast said.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they were eating, Jesus, having taken [the] bread and blessed, broke [it] and gave [it] to the disciples, and said, Take, eat: this is my body.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having taken [the] cup and given thanks, he gave [it] to them, saying, Drink ye all of it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this is my blood, that of the [new] covenant, that shed for many for remission of sins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I say to you, that I will not at all drink henceforth of this fruit of the vine, until that day when I drink it new with you in the kingdom of my Father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having sung a hymn, they went out to the mount of Olives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then saith Jesus to them, All ye shall be offended in me during this night. For it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock shall be scattered abroad.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But after that I shall be risen, I will go before you to Galilee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter answering said to him, If all shall be offended in thee, I will never be offended.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus said to him, Verily I say to thee, that during this night, before [the] cock shall crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Peter says to him, If I should needs die with thee, I will in no wise deny thee. Likewise said all the disciples also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Jesus comes with them to a place called Gethsemane, and says to the disciples, Sit here until I go away and pray yonder.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And taking with [him] Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, he began to be sorrowful and deeply depressed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he says to them, My soul is very sorrowful even unto death; remain here and watch with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And going forward a little he fell upon his face, praying and saying, My Father, if it be possible let this cup pass from me; but not as I will, but as thou [wilt].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he comes to the disciples and finds them sleeping, and says to Peter, Thus ye have not been able to watch one hour with me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Watch and pray, that ye enter not into temptation: the spirit indeed [is] ready, but the flesh weak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Again going away a second time he prayed saying, My Father, if this cannot pass [from me] unless I drink it, thy will be done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And coming he found them again sleeping, for their eyes were heavy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And leaving them, he went away again and prayed the third time, saying the same thing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he comes to the disciples and says to them, Sleep on now and take your rest; behold, the hour has drawn nigh, and the Son of man is delivered up into the hands of sinners.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise, let us go; behold, he that delivers me up has drawn nigh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And while he was yet speaking, behold, Judas, one of the twelve, came, and with him a great crowd with swords and sticks from the chief priests and elders of the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now he that delivered him up had given them a sign, saying, Whomsoever I shall kiss, he it is: seize him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately coming up to Jesus he said, Hail, Rabbi, and covered him with kisses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said to him, [My] friend, for what purpose art thou come? Then coming up they laid hands upon Jesus and seized him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, one of those with Jesus stretched out his hand and drew his sword, and smiting the bondman of the high priest took off his ear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then saith Jesus to him, Return thy sword to its place; for all who take the sword shall perish by the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or thinkest thou that I cannot now call upon my Father, and he will furnish me more than twelve legions of angels?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How then should the scriptures be fulfilled that thus it must be?

translations.name: Darby Bible
