translations.text: In that hour Jesus said to the crowds, Are ye come out as against a robber with swords and sticks to take me? I sat daily [with you] teaching in the temple, and ye did not seize me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But all this is come to pass that the scriptures of the prophets may be fulfilled. Then all the disciples left him and fled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now they that had seized Jesus led [him] away to Caiaphas the high priest, where the scribes and the elders were assembled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter followed him at a distance, even to the palace of the high priest, and entering in sat with the officers to see the end.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the chief priests and the elders and the whole sanhedrim sought false witness against Jesus, so that they might put him to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they found none, though many false witnesses came forward. But at the last two false witnesses came forward

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said, He said, I am able to destroy the temple ofGod, and in three days build it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the high priest standing up said to him, Answerest thou nothing? What do these witness against thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus was silent. And the high priest answering said to him, I adjure thee by the livingGod that thou tell us if thou art the Christ the Son ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus says to him, Thou hast said. Moreover, I say to you, From henceforth ye shall see the Son of man sitting at the right hand of power, and coming on the clouds of heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the high priest rent his clothes, saying, He has blasphemed: what need have we any more of witnesses? behold, now ye have heard the blasphemy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What think ye? And they answering said, He is liable to the penalty of death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then they spit in his face, and buffeted him, and some struck him with the palms of their hand,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, Prophesy to us, Christ, Who is it who struck thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Peter sat without in the palace-court; and a maid came to him, saying, And thou wast with Jesus the Galilaean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he denied before all, saying, I do not know what thou sayest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he had gone out into the entrance, another [maid] saw him, and says to those there, This [man] also was with Jesus the Nazaraean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And again he denied with an oath: I do not know the man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after a little, those who stood [there], coming to [him], said to Peter, Truly thou too art of them, for also thy speech makes thee manifest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he began to curse and to swear, I know not the man. And immediately [the] cock crew.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter remembered the word of Jesus, who had said [to him], Before [the] cock crow thou shalt deny me thrice. And he went forth without, and wept bitterly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when it was morning all the chief priests and the elders of the people took counsel against Jesus so that they might put him to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having bound him they led him away, and delivered him up to Pontius Pilate, the governor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Judas, who delivered him up, seeing that he had been condemned, filled with remorse, returned the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and the elders,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, I have sinned [in] having delivered up guiltless blood. But they said, What is that to us? see thou [to that].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having cast down the pieces of silver in the temple, he left the place, and went away and hanged himself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the chief priests took the pieces of silver and said, It is not lawful to cast them into the Corban, since it is [the] price of blood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having taken counsel, they bought with them the field of the potter for a burying-ground for strangers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore that field has been called Blood-field unto this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then was fulfilled that which was spoken through Jeremias the prophet, saying, And I took the thirty pieces of silver, the price of him that was set a price on, whom [they who were] of the sons of Israel had set a price on,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they gave them for the field of the potter, according as [the] Lord commanded me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus stood before the governor. And the governor questioned him, saying, Art thou the King of the Jews? And Jesus said to him, Thou sayest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he was accused of the chief priests and the elders, he answered nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then says Pilate to him, Hearest thou not how many things they witness against thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he answered him not so much as one word, so that the governor wondered exceedingly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now at [the] feast the governor was accustomed to release one prisoner to the crowd, whom they would.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they had then a notable prisoner, named Barabbas.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They therefore being gathered together, Pilate said to them, Whom will ye that I release to you, Barabbas, or Jesus who is called Christ?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he knew that they had delivered him up through envy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But, as he was sitting on the judgment-seat, his wife sent to him, saying, Have thou nothing to do with that righteous [man]; for I have suffered to-day many things in a dream because of him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the crowds that they should beg for Barabbas, and destroy Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the governor answering said to them, Which of the two will ye that I release unto you? And they said, Barabbas.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pilate says to them, What then shall I do with Jesus, who is called Christ? They all say, Let him be crucified.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the governor said, What evil then has he done? But they cried more than ever, saying, Let him be crucified.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pilate, seeing that it availed nothing, but that rather a tumult was arising, having taken water, washed his hands before the crowd, saying, I am guiltless of the blood of this righteous one: see ye [to it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the people answering said, His blood [be] on us and on our children.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he released to them Barabbas; but Jesus, having scourged [him], he delivered up that he might be crucified.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the soldiers of the governor, having taken Jesus with [them] to the praetorium, gathered against him the whole band,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having taken off his garment, put on him a scarlet cloak;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having woven a crown out of thorns, they put it on his head, and a reed in his right hand; and, bowing the knee before him, they mocked him, saying, Hail, King of the Jews!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having spit upon him, they took the reed and beat [him] on his head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they had mocked him, they took the cloak off him, and put his own clothes on him, and led him away to crucify.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they went forth they found a man of Cyrene, Simon by name; him they compelled to go [with them] that he might bear his cross.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having come to a place called Golgotha, which means Place of a skull,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: they gave to him to drink vinegar mingled with gall; and having tasted [it], he would not drink.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having crucified him, they parted his clothes amongst [themselves], casting lots.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And sitting down, they kept guard over him there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they set up over his head his accusation written: This is Jesus, the King of the Jews.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then are crucified with him two robbers, one on the right hand and one on the left.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the passers-by reviled him, shaking their heads

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and saying, Thou that destroyest the temple and buildest it in three days, save thyself. If thou art Son ofGod, descend from the cross.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [And] in like manner the chief priests also, mocking, with the scribes and elders, said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He saved others, himself he cannot save. He is King of Israel: let him descend now from the cross, and we will believe on him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He trusted uponGod; let him save him now if he will [have] him. For he said, I am Son ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the robbers also who had been crucified with him cast the same reproaches on him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now from [the] sixth hour there was darkness over the whole land until [the] ninth hour;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but about the ninth hour Jesus cried out with a loud voice, saying, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani? that is, MyGod, myGod, why hast thou forsaken me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And some of those who stood there, when they heard [it], said, This [man] calls for Elias.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately one of them running and getting a sponge, having filled [it] with vinegar and fixed [it] on a reed, gave him to drink.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the rest said, Let be; let us see if Elias comes to save him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, having again cried with a loud voice, gave up the ghost.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And lo, the veil of the temple was rent in two from the top to the bottom, and the earth was shaken, and the rocks were rent,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the tombs were opened; and many bodies of the saints fallen asleep arose,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and going out of the tombs after his arising, entered into the holy city and appeared unto many.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the centurion, and they who were with him on guard over Jesus, seeing the earthquake and the things that took place, feared greatly, saying, Truly this [man] was Son ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there were there many women beholding from afar off, who had followed Jesus from Galilee ministering to him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: among whom was Mary of Magdala, and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now when even was come there came a rich man of Arimathaea, his name Joseph, who also himself was a disciple to Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He, going to Pilate, begged the body of Jesus. Then Pilate commanded the body to be given up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Joseph having got the body, wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and laid it in his new tomb which he had hewn in the rock; and having rolled a great stone to the door of the tomb, went away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Mary of Magdala was there, and the other Mary, sitting opposite the sepulchre.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now on the morrow, which is after the preparation, the chief priests and the Pharisees came together to Pilate,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, Sir, we have called to mind that that deceiver said when he was still alive, After three days I arise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Command therefore that the sepulchre be secured until the third day, lest his disciples should come and steal him away, and say to the people, He is risen from the dead; and the last error shall be worse than the first.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pilate said to them, Ye have a watch: go, secure it as well as ye know how.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they went and secured the sepulchre, having sealed the stone, with the watch [besides].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now late on sabbath, as it was the dusk of the next day after sabbath, came Mary of Magdala and the other Mary to look at the sepulchre.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, there was a great earthquake; for an angel of [the] Lord, descending out of heaven, came and rolled away the stone and sat upon it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his look was as lightning, and his clothing white as snow.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And for fear of him the guards trembled and became as dead men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the angel answering said to the women, Fear not ye, for I know that ye seek Jesus the crucified one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He is not here, for he is risen, as he said. Come, see the place where the Lord lay.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And go quickly and say to his disciples that he is risen from the dead; and behold, he goes before you into Galilee, there shall ye see him. Behold, I have told you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And going out quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, they ran to bring his disciples word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they went to bring his disciples word, behold also, Jesus met them, saying, Hail! And they coming up took him by the feet, and did him homage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Jesus says to them, Fear not; go, bring word to my brethren that they go into Galilee, and there they shall see me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they went, behold, some of the watch went into the city, and brought word to the chief priests of all that had taken place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having assembled with the elders, and having taken counsel, they gave a large sum of money to the soldiers,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, Say that his disciples coming by night stole him [while] we [were] sleeping.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if this should come to the hearing of the governor, we will persuade him, and save you from all anxiety.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took the money and did as they had been taught. And this report is current among the Jews until this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the eleven disciples went into Galilee to the mountain which Jesus had appointed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they saw him, they did homage to him: but some doubted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus coming up spoke to them, saying, All power has been given me in heaven and upon earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go [therefore] and make disciples of all the nations, baptising them to the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Spirit;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have enjoined you. And behold, I am with you all the days, until the completion of the age.
